# connectracer_connect_rule (Resource)

Manages an AWS Connect Rule resource.

Rules allow you to trigger actions based on events in your Amazon Connect instance, such as contact events, agent events, and more.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `actions_json` (String) A JSON-encoded string representing the array of rule actions. Each action object must include an `action_type` field and the corresponding action definition
- `event_source_name` (String) The name of the event source. The trigger event source cannot be changed after creation
- `function` (String) The conditions of the rule (rule function as a JSON-like string)
- `instance_id` (String) The identifier of the Amazon Connect instance
- `name` (String) The name of the rule
- `publish_status` (String) The publish status of the rule. Valid values: `DRAFT`, `PUBLISHED`

### Optional

- `integration_association_id` (String) The identifier for the integration association (only needed for certain event sources)
- `tags` (Map of String) Tags to apply to the rule

### Read-Only

- `created_time` (String) The timestamp when the rule was created (RFC3339 format)
- `id` (String) The identifier for the rule (RuleId)
- `last_updated_by` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who last updated the rule
- `last_updated_time` (String) The timestamp when the rule was last updated (RFC3339 format)
- `rule_arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the rule
